000075.notes: [Updated by AI]: Latte, symmetric tulip pour (progression from 3-leaf tulip on #71). Coffee came through balanced but mild/bland in the cup. Same recipe as #66/#67/#71 (18g → 38g, DF64 9.5, 91°C, James Huehuetenango [AI]). Consider +1°C to 92°C or 1:1.7 ratio if milkiness continues to flatten flavor.

000076.notes: [Updated by AI]: Fuller body, stronger/darker character than Huehue. Less red berry lift than the README forecast — chocolate-leaning. Yield overshot target (40.6g vs 38g target = 1:2.26 vs 1:2.11). Solid working baseline.

000077.notes: [Updated by AI]: Fuller body, stronger flavor. +1°C bump (93→94) unlocked the depth. Same yield as shot 76 (40.4g), but at 94°C target the cup is properly developed. Dialed in — this is the working profile.

000078.notes: [Updated by AI]: Cortado attempt. A bit sour but overall satisfying. SCALE ANOMALY: scale was bouncing pre-shot with empty cup (Gibraltar glass may be heavier than typical cup) — predictive stop fired on bad data. Reported 45g out is unreliable. Don't change profile/grind based on this shot — repeat with scale tared and verified first.

000080.notes: [Updated by AI]: First neat espresso from this Colombia bag. Sour but full body and nice finish — 4★ overall. Same recipe as 5★ shot 77 (DF64 9.5, 18g, 94°C profile). Scale behaved cleanly today (regular shot glass, not the heavy Gibraltar). Hypotheses for the sourness vs shot 77's balance: (a) first shot of morning = cold brass shower under-extracts, (b) scale worked = yield landed at target ~38g instead of yesterday's overshoot to 40g, less mask = more acidity, (c) tasting neat for first time vs in Americano/cortado where milk/water mask acidity. Telemetry timed out — yield estimated.

000083.notes: [Updated by AI]: Latte w/ Califia oat milk. Thin body, no sweetness, short finish. Shot too thin for milk context — need shorter ratio (~33g) for milk drinks. Oat milk technique: over-stretched (5-6s dairy habit), large bubbles dissolved on pour. Fix: 2-3s stretch max, roll to 60-62°C.

000087.notes: [Updated by AI]: Clean balanced shot; medium body, present sweetness, clean finish; aerocano
000087.bean_type: James Coffee Co — Colombia Washed Finca Palmichal
